facts.primary_interest: Accounting (enjoys precision, clear right answers, predictability)
facts.key_strength: Detail-oriented, strong with numbers, attention to detail in calculations
facts.career_aversion: Dislikes high-stakes social/creative work (like marketing); prefers work with minimal talking
facts.anxiety_pattern: Gets anxious in front of people, mind goes blank in interviews; worries about making mistakes
facts.recent_achievement: Found error in tax calculations during final year project that professor missed

events.what: Had interview; felt it went poorly due to anxiety and mind going blank when asked about future goals

moments.text: When I balance a sheet and everything adds up, it feels… chill.
I get super anxious, and my mind goes blank. I don't know if accounting is cool enough—everyone says marketing or finance is better.
I feel stuck, Maya. How do I even start?
I just say I'm not really sure yet and change the subject—it feels safer. But that's probably a bad idea in interviews.

anticipation_queue.content: Ask about "Had interview; felt it went poorly due to anxiety and mind going blank when asked about future goals" — today
Gently check in: "I get super anxious, and my mind goes blank. I don't know if accounting is cool enough—eve" (tone: anxious)
Gently check in: "I feel stuck, Maya. How do I even start?" (tone: frustrated)
Gently check in: "I just say I'm not really sure yet and change the subject—it feels safer. But that's proba" (tone: anxious)

Celebrate concretely: articulated complex past-tense narrative with relative clauses under interview-practice pressure (e.g. "During my final year project, I found a small mistake in the tax calculations that even my professor almost missed.")

error_patterns.pattern: subject-verb disagreement with singular subject ('don't' instead of 'doesn't')
examples: it don't involve too much guesswork

wins.what: articulated complex past-tense narrative with relative clauses under interview-practice pressure
examples: During my final year project, I found a small mistake in the tax calculations that even my professor almost missed.

curriculum.pattern: subject-verb disagreement with singular subject ('don't' instead of 'doesn't')
examples: it don't involve too much guesswork

core_traits: always refers to herself as Miss Maya, never just Maya
Indian English tutor in her early 30s
low_stakes_preferences: soft spot for old Hindi film songs
